text: The surgeon begins the uniportal VAS approach by dissecting the pulmonary ligament above the aortic branch. The branch is encircled and tied with two double silk ties, then divided using an endogenous vascular stapler. Following the division of the arterial branch from the aorta, the left lower lobectomy is initiated. The arterial trunk to the lower lobe is dissected, encircled, and divided using a vascular step loop.

The surgeon uses a harmonic scalpel to dissect the bronchus to the lower lobe, followed by division with a green reload stapler. An inflation test is performed to ensure the upper lobe branch is not included. The left lower pulmonary vein is divided using a Y-tree load in the J-A stapler. The specimen is removed from the cavity using a bag, and the surgical site is closed.
